title: taxus × media
intro: Taxus × media, also known as yew, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the yew family (Taxaceae) and is a cross between English yew (Taxus baccata) and Japanese yew (Taxus cuspidata). This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including containing taxine, a toxic alkaloid with anti-inflammatory and antineoplastic effects. The health benefits of Taxus × media include its potential to prevent cancer cell growth, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Taxus × media are available in the form of capsules, tablets, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and reduce the risk of certain diseases.
